comments.author: @Earthcue
comments.text: Dragons are real — and they live in Indonesia! 🐉 The Komodo dragon is the world's largest lizard, growing up to 3 meters long and 135kg heavy, with a venomous bite. They even have their own UNESCO-protected island: Komodo Island. Imagine hiking and suddenly meeting one of these prehistoric giants face to face! 😱

comments.author: @andrewwalker7439
comments.text: Currently living in this beautiful country with its astounding culture,it truly is mesmerising with literally a surprise around every corner

comments.author: @demus8757
comments.text:
When will Americans ever learn to pronounce foreign names the way they should be pronounced? Answer: NEVER. Reason: they know it better. Well, they think they know it better. It sounds so ridiculous. Better set the volume to zero if you want to enjoy the video.
I managed to make through to the end but I really don't understand this video. Some parts, which are less important are shown extremely long, other parts are not shown at all, then it is back to a part which has been shown before, in other words it is a disaster. I love Indonesia, I come there for over 40 years now, not every year although if I add up the amount of times I have been there it will be close to 40 thanks to visiting 2 times per year. This video does not show Indonesia as it could be shown. If the trips you sell are just as bad then I wonder if you have any clients at all.

comments.author: @The-Global-travel
comments.text:
is video is absolutely mind-blowing! 🌍🔥
Indonesia truly feels unreal with its extreme landscapes—from powerful volcanoes to deep jungles and stunning islands. Every scene is intense, beautiful, and full of adventure
